heading: Requirements for issuers of qualified long-term care insurance contracts

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: There is hereby imposed on any person failing to meet the requirements of subsection (c) or (d) a tax in the amount determined under subsection (b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount of the tax imposed by subsection (a) shall be $100 per insured for each day any requirement of subsection (c) or (d) is not met with respect to each qualified long-term care insurance contract.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 2 Waiver
children.text: In the case of a failure which is due to reasonable cause and not to willful neglect, the Secretary may waive part or all of the tax imposed by subsection (a) to the extent that payment of the tax would be excessive relative to the failure involved.

children.label: i
children.text: Section 13 (relating to application forms and replacement coverage).

children.label: ii
children.text: Section 14 (relating to reporting requirements), except that the issuer shall also report at least annually the number of claims denied during the reporting period for each class of business (expressed as a percentage of claims denied), other than claims denied for failure to meet the waiting period or because of any applicable preexisting condition.

children.label: iii
children.text: Section 20 (relating to filing requirements for marketing).

children.label: I
children.text: in addition to such requirements, no person shall, in selling or offering to sell a qualified long-term care insurance contract, misrepresent a material fact; and

children.label: II
children.text: no such requirements shall include a requirement to inquire or identify whether a prospective applicant or enrollee for long-term care insurance has accident and sickness insurance.

children.label: v
children.text: Section 22 (relating to appropriateness of recommended purchase).

children.label: vi
children.text: Section 24 (relating to standard format outline of coverage).

children.label: vii
children.text: Section 25 (relating to requirement to deliver shopper’s guide).

children.label: i
children.text: Section 6F (relating to right to return), except that such section shall also apply to denials of applications and any refund shall be made within 30 days of the return or denial.

children.label: ii
children.text: Section 6G (relating to outline of coverage).

children.label: iii
children.text: Section 6H (relating to requirements for certificates under group plans).

children.label: iv
children.text: Section 6I (relating to policy summary).

children.label: v
children.text: Section 6J (relating to monthly reports on accelerated death benefits).

children.label: vi
children.text: Section 7 (relating to incontestability period).

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: C Definitions
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the terms “model regulation” and “model Act” have the meanings given such terms by section 7702B(g)(2)(B).

children.heading: Delivery of policy
children.label: 2 Delivery of policy
children.text: If an application for a qualified long-term care insurance contract (or for a certificate under such a contract for a group) is approved, the issuer shall deliver to the applicant (or policyholder or certificateholder) the contract (or certificate) of insurance not later than 30 days after the date of the approval.

children.label: A
children.text: provide a written explanation of the reasons for the denial, and

children.label: B
children.text: make available all information directly relating to such denial.

children.heading: Disclosure
children.label: d Disclosure
children.text: The requirements of this subsection are met if the issuer of a long-term care insurance policy discloses in such policy and in the outline of coverage required under subsection (c)(1)(B)(ii) that the policy is intended to be a qualified long-term care insurance contract under section 7702B(b).

children.heading: Qualified long-term care insurance contract defined
children.label: e Qualified long-term care insurance contract defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “qualified long-term care insurance contract” has the meaning given such term by section 7702B.

children.heading: Coordination with State requirements
children.label: f Coordination with State requirements
children.text: If a State imposes any requirement which is more stringent than the analogous requirement imposed by this section or section 7702B(g), the requirement imposed by this section or section 7702B(g) shall be treated as met if the more stringent State requirement is met.
